title: Vũ Điệu Của Tội Lỗi

'''Vũ Điệu Của Tội Lỗi''' là màn 5 [[Chương 4]] [[Nhiệm Vụ Ma Thần]].

==Danh Sách Nhiệm Vụ==
# [[Thảm Họa Thủy Triều]]
# [[Tương Phùng Cũng Là Ly Biệt]]

<!--
==Tóm Lược==
;Deluge of Wrathful Waters

At the [[Fortress of Meropide]], the [[Traveler]] and [[Paimon]] wake up; aware that their "sentence" is over, they head out to sign the release papers with [[Monglane]]. During the process, the prison shakes, surprising everyone, so the Traveler decides to talk to [[Wriothesley]] if he knew anything about it. Wriothesley reminds them that the seal that [[Neuvillette]] had [[Calamitous Tread|placed earlier]] would not fail, and if it did, the inmates would be in a panic; rather, the tremor came from the surface. After seeing the two off, they head to the [[Palais Mermonia]] to speak to Neuvillette.

When they arrive, the place is buzzing with activity; [[Sedene]] spots them, but as they're welcomed guests, lets them enter Neuvillette's office. Inside, they speak to Neuvillette once he finishes his work, informing him of the [[All-Devouring Narwhal|narwhal-like entity]] they saw from [[Childe's Vision]]. He also wonders as to how he ended up in the [[Primordial Sea]], since people aren't normally able to enter it. They then ask him about the tremor, which he confirms happened in [[Poisson]]; after the tremor, the water levels rose quickly before they receded. However, he suspects that the water might have been related to the Primordial Sea and thus stays behind to form disaster prevention plans, asking the two to go in his stead.

The two arrive at Poission where they find the city devoid of normal activity as [[Spina di Rosula]] members rush to aid the wounded. They eventually find [[Navia]], who tells them that after the tremor, water began rushing out, soon revealed to be Primordial Water and dissolved everyone on the lower levels and causing panic to everyone else. The Traveler then realizes that people are missing and asks about [[Melus]] and [[Silver]], but before she could answer, another member requires her attention. Once the situation is sorted out, she asks them to accompany her to her [[Callas|father's]] grave. There, she breaks down, revealing that when she initiated the rescue plan, the two went to save people, but were caught in the waters and dissolved. She laments that she could not hold a funeral for them, as they had previously requested for her to bury them by Callas' side when the time came. Navia then hands them a list of the victims, which she considered fortunate for the costs. The two reassure her that she shouldn't see it that way and Paimon tells her to think of Melus and Silver as heroes, which she appreciates.

[[The Knave|Arlecchino]] then walks in to discuss the arrangements she made with her. As they were in the area, she had the Fatui lend them a hand in the relief efforts. Navia apologizes for testing Arlecchino, since she wanted to know who she was working with at the time, while also revealing that Arlecchino had set her subordinates to plenty of work with supplies, water levels and headcounts. Arlecchino sends her regards for Melus' and Silver's demise, but before she leaves, asks the Traveler for assistance. She reveals that the [[House of the Hearth]] had learned that a child stumbled upon some ancient ruins near Poisson; as such, she had sent her subordinates for ruin exploration, but with the disaster on hand, had them assist in relief efforts. The Traveler accepts the task and Navia decides to tag along to keep her mind off her friends' deaths.

;Meeting Is Also Parting

Arriving at the ruins, they quickly discover the area is flooded with Primordial Water. Paimon initially suggests that Navia head back, but upon realizing there isn't a path back, she decides to continue forward with them. As they walk across a bridge, it suddenly gives way; Navia slips after a jump, prompting the Traveler to turn back and grab her, but she fails and she falls into the water. Navia then wakes up in Poisson with Melus and Silver, but quickly realizes something is off. The two then escort her to the [[Opera Epiclese]] for a trial of which she is the defendant. The prosecutor, [[Joinville]], declares her guilty and the audience demands she joins them, while Melus and Silver realize something is off and defend her. Navia then realizes that everyone present were victims of the disaster, but before the audience could take her with them, Neuvillette appears and dismisses them. Melus and Silver say their final goodbyes to Navia as Neuvillette brings her back to reality, where she reveals her dream to the three. Neuvillette explains that there had just been a cave-in, but saw Navia being protected by two Oceanids, which was long enough for him to rescue her.

Navia is humbled that the two would continue protecting her even after death, while Neuvillette pulls the Traveler and Paimon aside as to give her some space. He reveals that he had come to Poisson to meet up with them, but was surprised to see the Fatui helping out and ran into Arlecchino, who told him where they were. The four then continue exploring the ruins, where they discover several stone slates on the wall, though one appears to be missing. Paimon reads the text, surprising her as it says that the nation must be destroyed. Looking at the second slate, they find several people kneeling while facing an [[Celestia|island in the sky]]. The third image shows Furina in the water, surrounded by people in the water, while the fourth image aligns with the prophecy of her being alone. They then realize something is off with the order of the images and deduce that the significant person in the second image is [[Egeria]], the previous Hydro Archon, who appears to be kneeling as if forgiveness for a sin. With no further leads to go on however, they leave the ruins, with Navia checking in on her organization while Neuvillette goes to confront Furina about the prophecy. Navia has the Traveler and Paimon rest at their headquarters in [[Court of Fontaine: Fleuve Cendre|Fleuve Cendre]].

;Hunters, Prophets

When the two arrive, the Traveler and Paimon are treated to a large meal by [[Tetreaux]] before [[Mona]] spots them. Mona had come to Fontaine for some business and reveals her master's name, [[Barbeloth]]. She insists that she is not a Fontanian, having been born in [[Mondstadt]]. The two ask her about the water, which she confirms as her main reason of coming after being invited by [[The Steambird]] to come over. She expresses concern about the prophecy due to it being "world-changing" and states that the other [[Hexenzirkel]] members could also discern it. The Traveler asks her to get in touch with her master to ascertain it, which she expresses surprise to but agrees.

The next day, they head to Neuvillette to see if he managed to gain any useful information from Furina. Upon arriving, Sedene informs them that the two are in an argument. Neuvillette asks Furina to stop dodging the question as he hands her a list of victims from the Poisson incident, not wanting it to happen again and that he went to some ancient ruins near the area where one of the stone slates was missing. Furina states that she and Egeria are two different people and thus she is not privy to her predecessor's secrets, she has a plan in motion and orders Neuvillette not to speak of the incident again as she darts from his office, failing to notice the Traveler. The Traveler then walks in and Neuvillette explains that as Furina is hiding secrets, states that the only way to make the truth come out is to put her on trial; while he would not like to do so, he has no choice given the circumstances. 

The Traveler contacts Navia, [[Lyney]], [[Lynette]], [[Freminet]] and [[Clorinde]] to meet up in Poisson with Neuvillette, where they discuss a plan to trap Furina and make her reveal her secrets to deal with the prophecy. When they return to Fleuve Cendre, they talk about their plan before someone sets a cup of tea down and joins them, though they're nowhere to be seen. The Traveler quickly realizes that it must be [[Mage "N"]] who tells them that even the gods' gaze has blind spots. The Traveler thinks about her words before going to sleep. When they wake up again, they find Mona, who had talked to Charlotte about the prophecy. They also tell her of N's visit, who remarks that the situation must truly be dire for her to talk to them in person. 

Paimon decides to take a walk around the city, where they stop at [[Hotel Debord]] to get a quick bite. Paimon notices the cake that was served in the tea party with Arlecchino and Furina and goes to order one, but Sedene suddenly appears and orders one too. Paimon asks her what she would do if the place closed down, and she tells her that she would continue her routine regardless. Seeing that they're sad, she buys two more slices of cake for them to lift their spirits up, having learned it from Sigewinne before leaving. They then go to the Steambird and meet up with Charlotte, who she asks the same question. Charlotte states that she would still continue her work; while she would leave, she would be duty-bound to stay as a journalist and not forsake her homeland that easily.

With the walk around the city down, Paimon decides to go out to sea and asks them the same question. The Traveler tells her that they would continue journeying and think of all the good times they had in Teyvat. They spend the next few days in Fleuve Cendre before [[Isadora]] arrives, telling them that there was a small-scale riot in the Opera Epiclese. During a show intermission, some people accused Furina of not doing anything for the "prophecy crisis". Before she could refute, others began joining in, causing her to leave while the [[Marechaussee Phantom]] dealt with the situation; once that was resolved, Furina was nowhere to be found. Knowing Furina, they head to Poisson to look for her.

;Apocalypse

There, they find Furina lamenting the victims of the recent disaster before several people appear to chase after her. They manage to lure them away with misdirection before entering a house, where Furina begins to unravel and admit that she had been investigating the prophecy, but to seemingly no effect as she could not make an enemy of the divine. The Traveler manages to convince her to inform her everything as a "witness", as they were not from Teyvat, but before she could say anything, the house shakes again, though it quickly collapses and reveals to be an elaborate opening act by Lyney to begin her trial. Furina finds the idea of being tried absurd, but when Neuvillette takes this as a refusal, the other option is a duel with Clorinde, which she refuses by raising both hands up, surprising the audience. Furina then clarifies that she is not completely surrendering, rather she is agreeing to go on trial for her own misdeeds of not protecting her people. 

The Traveler serves as the prosecutor while Furina serves as the defendant, knowing that they could get the truth if they could best her in court. Before they start, Furina asks them how much time they spent to force her on the stage and learns the extent and role everyone played in doing so; Navia's subordinates were meant to lure her away both at the opera and at Poisson, while the Fatui built a "Giant Magic Box" to replicate Lyney's original trick and tunneled all the way to the opera house, leaving Clorinde ready to duel her should she refuse trial. Furina then assumes that the Traveler was to only prosecute her, but they tell her that they wanted to give her one last chance to admit everything. As the stage was already set, Furina states that the only action left was to begin the trial.

The Traveler presses the charge of Furina being a fraud, surprising everyone due to the blatancy of the accusation. Despite this, they manage to convince the audience through supporting information complied by Charlotte, culminating with Navia providing a sample of Primordial Water for her to touch. Furina is initially hesitant to touch the water, but does so to maintain her innocence. Neuvillette then calls Sigewinne in for a check-up, who reveals that she shared the same symptoms as other Fontanians exposed to impure Primordial Water. The audience is fully convinced and Nevuillette concludes the trial, deeming her guilty and leaving her in tears. Neuvillette then asks the [[Oratrice Mecanique d'Analyse Cardinale]] for the final verdict, which also declares her guilty, but also demands the Hydro Archon to be punished via death sentence, shocking everyone. Neuvillette remarks that the sentence is overly excessive and that it sentenced the Hydro Archon, not her. Suddenly, Freminet appears with the missing stone slate.

Neuvillette analyzes the slate and asks the Traveler to come down to interpret them, where they discover Egeria pouring her power to turn the Oceanids into humans; Fontaine's people. Using what they learned from the other slates, they deduce the truth of the prophecy; the dissolved would return as an Oceanid, Celestia declared that the Hydro Archon would be killed for her "sin", Navia's near-death experience was a "trial" meant to make her stay and the eruption of the Primordial Sea at the Fortress of Meropide meant that the prophecy would eventually be fulfilled. The Traveler gives their hypothesis of the prophecy, which impresses Neuvillette. He then reveals that there was another truth hidden, which he was unable to decipher at the time as they only had three slates. 

In the Fontaine of old, Egeria's Oceanid followers yearned to live on land, but Egeria did not have the ability to create life herself. Thus, she used the Primordial Sea to grant them humanoid bodies by pouring it into their blood vessels; thus, if the water made contact with them, they would "dissolve" back into their original forms. As she did not ask for permission from the Heavenly Principles, they were thus "sinned" for appropriating its power, leaving the audience baffled at the truth. He then explains that the Traveler's hypothesis was incorrect, as the slates were in position; the third showed people in the water, but the water was not from the Primordial Sea; rather, it symbolized judgement and justice, and thus, the people of Fontaine would put the Hydro Archon on trial at the court of justice. The fourth slate was actually a sign of things to come - the  entity they saw in Childe's dream. 

Suddenly, the ground shakes as a large rift opens in the opera house, causing the massive narwhal-like entity the Traveler saw earlier to emerge; Neuvillette briefly attacks it before it focuses on the audience. Before it could consume them, Childe, in his [[Foul Legacy Transformation|Foul Legacy]] form, appears and works with Neuvillette to push it back into the rift, which he also falls into. Neuvillette, seeing the monster first-hand, realizes that the monster had roamed the Primordial Sea for ages and was responsible for the rising water levels. Once it consumed all it could, it would then target any remaining sources on Teyvat - the people of Fontaine. However, its appearance had been delayed by Childe fighting it, though it would come back soon after recovering and could not be stopped as it was basically the sea itself. Recalling N's words, the Traveler realizes there would be a way to fulfill the prophecy and save everyone at the same time. The Oratrice prepares to hand out the sentence, causing the Traveler to reach out to Furina; one of her tears lands on their hands, which sends Neuvillette and the Traveler into different realms.

;The Opera of Noirceur and Blancheur

Inside the his alternate realm, Neuvillette encounters Focalors, asking her why she deceived them. Focalors explains that she did so to deceive the Heavenly Principles, as she had been thrust into the situation by Egeria. After much thought, she realized the only way to save her people would be to outwit them. Focalors herself was also a transformed human, originally being an Oceanid. When she ascended as an Archon, she split her divinity in body and spirit, leaving Furina as her "body" and herself as "spirit". Furina was everything she wanted as a human, but to maintain her ploy, she cursed her. To fulfill the prophecy, she invited Furina to play as an actress. The "curse" she placed on her was that as long as her divinity existed, she could not die, but not able to live her life in the pursuit of happiness, thus she was forced to play her role in the opera for 500 years. 

The Traveler sees what Furina had gone through in the past 500 years while Focalors explains to Neuvillette about her plan and her intended sacrifice. As she executes herself, her Gnosis is returned to a shocked and grieving Neuvillette, while she thanks Furina for her role and wishes for her to live as a human. Neuvillette, with his full power restored, absolves the sins of all Fontanians, thus making the prophecy fulfilled, but making them immune to dissipation. Returning to the real realm, he tells the group that they can finally tackle the beast, as he can separate the water it accumulated. As the Oratrice is no longer functioning, he asks the Traveler to accompany him as his executor. 

Entering the Primordial Sea, Neuvillette and the Traveler subdue the beast. Before they could do anything else, a woman appears and uses a strange power to convert the beast into an orb and hurls both it and Childe in the rift she came out of. She remarks on how her disciple and her "master's pet" began brawling with each other. Paimon recognizes her as [[Skirk]], Childe's master. She tells them that she does not usually talk to the weak and saw them as equals when they defeated the narwhal without using the "power beyond this world". They ask her of her master, who she calls [[Surtalogi]], though they do not know that name either. She then asks about [[Vedrfolnir]] or [[Rhinedottir]], of which Paimon recognizes the latter, though Skirk admits had only heard of them. Skirk informs them that the narwhal used all its strength to fight them and that the prophecy would still occur; however, due to Focalors' deception and Neuvillette having absolved them of sin, the people do not dissolve and the waters quickly recede.

;Finale
With the prophecy averted, the people of Fontaine were busy rebuilding their society while the Steambird was printing out free copies to calm the masses down. Charlotte decides to head to Poisson to check in on the rebuilding efforts, having the Traveler tag along. Poisson is busy rebuilding after the disaster, with help from the Fatui and Clorinde, who had come to lay low as her reputation skyrocketed after challenging Furina to a duel. Once she finishes up all her business in the town, she heads to the Fortress of Meropide for an interview with [[Jurieu]] and [[Lourvine]] due to their flying ship. Finally, they head to [[Romaritime Harbor]], catching up with Lyney, Lynette and Freminet. Lyney tells them that "Father" was given the Hydro Gnosis and that Childe was recovering from his injuries. Paimon remarks on how they've allied with the Fatui for the first time, prompting Arlecchino to appear and surprise them. She states that as a Harbinger, it's her duty to give the Gnosis to the [[Tsaritsa]]. The Traveler also hands her Childe's Vision, which she accepts to return it to him.

After seeing both groups off, the Traveler and Paimon look for Neuvillette, who's waiting outside the [[Erinnyes]] entrance to the prison. He tells them everything that happened and that with his fully restored powers, he deemed Fontanians "true humans", preventing them from being dissolved upon contact with the Primordial Sea. After some time passed, Furina abdicated from her role as Hydro Archon and left the related affairs to Neuvillette before leaving the Opera. Neuvillette would ensure that her needs would be taken care of. He also reveals that after sending the two back to Teyvat, he spoke with Skirk further, who revealed that he had the "remains" of the third [[Descender]] on them and suggested he remove the Gnosis, as it would bring bad luck. Neuvillette tried to ask about their identity and when they died; she does not know, but she does promise to ask her master and have Tartaglia relay it to him if she does get one. Neuvillette also reveals his reasoning behind giving the Fatui the Hydro Gnosis to them.

With this information, the Traveler informs Neuvillette that they're the fourth Descender, leaving him impressed as he had not expected them to be one. They are still unsure as to what the Gnoses actually are, though they decide to ask Childe rather than make blind guesses. Neuvillette then ponders the gods' will and the difference between the rain that falls on everyone. He assumes the Traveler and Paimon are going to [[Natlan]], the land of endless war and a nation where both human and dragon coexist. The intel gained from the negotiation with The Knave also reveals that [[Il Capitano]] has "thrown his hat" into the endless ring of war in there. Neuvillette suggests them to fully prepare themselves before going. 
-->
